title: mearnsia regelii
intro: Mearnsia regelii, also known as the Indian snakeroot or Chota Chandan, is a plant native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. It is a shrub with yellow flowers and has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Mearnsia regelii include reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Mearnsia regelii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or used in Ayurvedic remedies like powders, pills, and ointments.
